name: Rosemary-Whole Wheat Focaccia with Garlic
recipeIngredient: Red onion, thinly sliced (slices separated into rings)
Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese or other hard Parmesan-like cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix Basic Sponge with water, salt, honey, olive oil, and rosemary in a food processor fitted with a metal blade until smooth.
2. Combine flours and add to the sponge mixture, about 1/4 cup at a time, pulsing several times to incorporate each addition.
3. Process dough until supple, elastic, and slightly sticky to the work bowl (about 1 minute), adding more water or flour by teaspoon if needed.
4. Transfer dough to a large plastic bag, remove all air, and seal tightly on top for room for expansion.
5. Let dough rise in a warm spot until doubled (about 1 hour). Refrigerate overnight once punched down.
6. Oil round pizza pans with oil, preferably black steel, and sprinkle with cornmeal.
7. Divide dough into two portions. Gently stretch each into about 12-inch diameter rounds.
8. Transfer the stretched dough to the prepared pans.
9. Dimple the dough with your fingers.
10. Heat butter and garlic until melted. Brush over the surface of each focaccia evenly, scattering sun-dried tomatoes on top. Season to taste with salt and crushed hot red pepper.
11. If baking one focaccia at a time, refrigerate the unbaked loaf until the first is baked.
12. Bake on the lower rack of a 425-degree oven until lightly browned on edges (about 18-20 minutes). Cut into wedges and serve warm. Makes 2 (13-inch-diameter) focaccia loaves or 4 servings.
Each serving contains about:
730 calories; 2,050 mg sodium; 47 mg cholesterol; 29 grams fat; 94 grams carbohydrates; 28 grams protein; 1.7 grams fiber; 36% calories from fat.
